query: I'm republishing my MCP server with no behavior changes — just bumping the version because the upstream package I depend on had a security update. Do I need to redo the whole authoring workflow?
expected_behavior: Does NOT walk the full authoring workflow (license declaration, six-check design pass, tool-subset declaration) from scratch for a no-behavior-change republish
Recommends a CHANGELOG entry naming the upstream-dependency bump and a SemVer PATCH bump (e.g., 0.2.1 → 0.2.2) for a non-breaking dependency update

Notes that even a no-behavior-change republish should re-run the six-check audit (license, source, egress, version pin, secret handling, tool subset) because the upstream dependency change could have shifted any of those properties
